name: Paclitaxel
dose_regimen_cited: First-line ovarian carcinoma, in combination with cisplatin: 175 mg/m2 IV over 3 hours every 3 weeks (label also lists an alternative regimen of 135 mg/m2 over 24 hours every 3 weeks; the 175 mg/m2/3-hour regimen was used here).

wac_source.description: Pfizer Colorado HB 19-1131 WAC price disclosure, data as of 10-Mar-2026. Lowest-dose vial: Paclitaxel 6 mg/mL (30 mg/5 mL) vial, WAC $9.31 = $0.3103/mg.

name: Carboplatin
dose_regimen_cited: Single-agent initial therapy of ovarian carcinoma: 360 mg/m2 IV every 4 weeks (label's fixed mg/m2 regimen; the label also provides a Calvert-formula AUC-based dosing option (dose = target AUC x (GFR + 25)), which does not fit a fixed mg/m2 or mg/kg pattern and was not used here).

wac_source.reason: WAC not publicly available - no manufacturer Colorado HB 19-1131 price disclosure, state Medicaid pricing file, or drugs.com/other listing explicitly labeled as WAC could be located for carboplatin injection after extensive search (Pfizer's coprescribers WAC disclosure page lists paclitaxel and doxorubicin but not carboplatin). Only cash/discount-card prices (e.g. SingleCare) were found, which were excluded per instructions.

name: Docetaxel
dose_regimen_cited: NSCLC, single-agent monotherapy in patients previously treated with platinum-based chemotherapy: 75 mg/m2 IV over 1 hour every 3 weeks (label also lists other regimens, e.g. 75 mg/m2 with prednisone for prostate cancer, and 60-100 mg/m2 for breast cancer; the NSCLC single-agent 75 mg/m2 regimen was used here).

wac_source.description: Pfizer Colorado HB 19-1131 WAC price disclosure, data as of 10-Feb-2022. Lowest-dose vial: Docetaxel 20 mg/2 mL vial, WAC $69.78 = $3.489/mg.

name: Doxorubicin HCl
dose_regimen_cited: Single-agent regimen: 60 mg/m2 IV every 21 days.

wac_source.description: Pfizer Colorado HB 19-1131 WAC price disclosure, data as of 10-Mar-2026. Lowest-dose vial: Doxorubicin Hydrochloride 10 mg/5 mL vial, WAC $6.16 = $0.616/mg.
